description: Jujutsu (jj) - A Git-compatible version control system that is both simple and powerful.

option.name: --restore-descendants
tip: Do not modify the content of the children of the abandoned commits

option.name: --retain-bookmarks
tip: Do not delete bookmarks pointing to the revisions to abandon

root.name: absorb
tip: Move changes from a revision into the stack of mutable revisions

option.name: --into
tip: Destination revisions to absorb into [default: mutable()]

option.name: --revisions
tip: If no revisions are specified, this defaults to the `revsets.arrange` setting, or `reachable(@, mutable())` if it is not set.

root.name: bisect
tip: Find a bad revision by bisection

next.name: run
tip: Run a given command to find the first bad revision

option.name: --find-good
tip: Whether to find the first good revision instead

next.name: advance
tip: Advance the closest bookmarks to a target revision

next.name: delete
tip: Delete an existing bookmark and propagate the deletion to remotes on the next push

next.name: forget
tip: Forget a bookmark without marking it as a deletion to be pushed

option.name: --include-remotes
tip: When forgetting a local bookmark, also forget any corresponding remote bookmarks

option.name: --all-remotes
tip: Show all tracking and non-tracking remote bookmarks including the ones whose targets are synchronized with the local bookmarks

option.name: --remote
tip: Show all tracking and non-tracking remote bookmarks belonging to this remote

option.name: --revisions
tip: Show bookmarks whose local targets are in the given revisions

option.name: --sort
tip: Sort bookmarks based on the given key (or multiple keys)

[possible values: name, name-, author-name, author-name-, author-email, author-email-, author-date, author-date-, committer-name, committer-name-, committer-email, committer-email-, committer-date, committer-date-]

option.name: --template
tip: Render each bookmark using the given template

option.name: --tracked
tip: Show remote tracked bookmarks only. Omits local Git-tracking bookmarks by default

next.name: move
tip: Move existing bookmarks to target revision

next.name: rename
tip: Rename `old` bookmark name to `new` bookmark name

next.name: set
tip: Create or update a bookmark to point to a certain commit

option.name: --allow-backwards
tip: Allow moving the bookmark backwards or sideways

root.name: commit
tip: Update the description and create a new change on top

option.name: --interactive
tip: Interactively choose which changes to include in the first commit

option.name: --message
tip: The change description to use (don't open editor)

option.name: --tool
tip: Specify diff editor to be used (implies --interactive)

next.name: edit
tip: Start an editor on a jj config file

next.name: get
tip: Get the value of a given config option

next.name: list
tip: List variables set in config files, along with their values

next.name: path
tip: Print the paths to the config files

next.name: set
tip: Update a config file to set the given option to a given value

next.name: unset
tip: Update a config file to unset the given option

root.name: describe
tip: Update the change description or other metadata

option.name: --editor
tip: Open an editor to edit the change description

option.name: --message
tip: The change description to use (don't open editor)

option.name: --stdin
tip: Read the change description from stdin

root.name: diff
tip: Compare file contents between two revisions

option.name: --color-words
tip: Show a word-level diff with changes indicated only by color

option.name: --context
tip: Number of lines of context to show

option.name: --ignore-space-change
tip: Ignore changes in amount of whitespace when comparing lines

option.name: --name-only
tip: For each path, show only its path

option.name: --stat
tip: Show a histogram of the changes

option.name: --summary
tip: For each path, show only whether it was modified, added, or deleted

option.name: --template
tip: Render each file diff entry using the given template

option.name: --types
tip: For each path, show only its type before and after

root.name: diffedit
tip: Touch up the content changes in a revision with a diff editor

next.name: --restore-descendants
tip: Preserve the content (not the diff) when rebasing descendants
When rebasing a descendant on top of the rewritten revision, its diff compared to its parent(s) is normally preserved, i.e. the same way that descendants are always rebased.
This flag makes it so the content/state is preserved instead of preserving the diff.

next.name: --revision
tip: Defaults to @ if neither --to nor --from are specified.

next.name: --tool
tip: Specify diff editor to be used

root.name: duplicate
tip: Create new changes with the same content as existing ones

option.name: --destination
tip: The revision(s) to duplicate onto (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --insert-after
tip: The revision(s) to insert after (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --insert-before
tip: The revision(s) to insert before (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

root.name: edit
tip: Sets the specified revision as the working-copy revision

root.name: evolution-log
tip: Show how a change has evolved over time

option.name: --color-words
tip: Show a word-level diff with changes indicated only by color

option.name: --context
tip: Number of lines of context to show

option.name: --ignore-space-change
tip: Ignore changes in amount of whitespace when comparing lines

option.name: --limit
tip: Limit number of revisions to show
Applied after revisions are reordered topologically, but before being reversed.

option.name: --name-only
tip: For each path, show only its path
Typically useful for shell commands like: `jj diff -r @- --name-only | xargs perl -pi -e's/OLD/NEW/g`

option.name: --no-graph
tip: Don't show the graph, show a flat list of revisions

option.name: --patch
tip: Show patch compared to the previous version of this change
If the previous version has different parents, it will be temporarily rebased to the parents of the new version, so the diff is not contaminated by unrelated changes.

option.name: --reversed
tip: Show revisions in the opposite order (older revisions first)

option.name: --stat
tip: Show a histogram of the changes

option.name: --summary
tip: For each path, show only whether it was modified, added, or deleted

option.name: --template
tip: Render each revision using the given template
All 0-argument methods of the [`CommitEvolutionEntry` type] are available as keywords in the template expression.
See [`jj help -k templates`] for more information.
If not specified, this defaults to the `templates.evolog` setting.

option.name: --tool
tip: A builtin format can also be specified as `:<name>`. For example, `--tool=:git` is equivalent to `--git`.

option.name: --types
tip: For each path, show only its type before and after
The diff is shown as two letters. The first letter indicates the type before and the second letter indicates the type after.
'-' indicates that the path was not present, 'F' represents a regular file, `L' represents a symlink, 'C' represents a conflict, and 'G' represents a Git submodule.

next.name: annotate
tip: Show the source change for each line of the target file

next.name: chmod
tip: Sets or removes the executable bit for paths in the repo

next.name: show
tip: Print contents of files in a revision

next.name: track
tip: Start tracking specified paths in the working copy

next.name: untrack
tip: Stop tracking specified paths in the working copy

root.name: fix
tip: Update files with formatting fixes or other changes

option.name: --include-unchanged-files
tip: Fix unchanged files in addition to changed ones.
If no paths are specified, all files in the repo will be fixed

option.name: --source
tip: Fix files in the specified revision(s) and their descendants.
If no revisions are specified, this defaults to the `revsets.fix` setting, or `reachable(@, mutable())` if it is not set

root.name: git
tip: Commands for working with Git remotes and the underlying Git repo

next.name: clone
tip: Create a new repo backed by a clone of a Git repo

option.name: --colocate
tip: Whether or not to colocate the Jujutsu repo with the git repo.
It is the default value, and can be omitted.

option.name: --depth
tip: Create a shallow clone of the given depth

option.name: --fetch-tags
tip: Unless otherwise specified, the initial clone will fetch all tags, while all subsequent fetches will only fetch included tags.

next.name: included
tip: Only fetch tags that point to objects that are already being transmitted

option.name: --no-colocate
tip: Disable colocation of the Jujutsu repo with the git repo

option.name: --remote
tip: Name of the newly created remote. [default: origin]

next.name: colocation
tip: Manage Jujutsu repository colocation with Git

next.name: disable
tip: Convert into a non-colocated Jujutsu/Git repository

next.name: enable
tip: Convert into a colocated Jujutsu/Git repository

next.name: export
tip: Update the underlying Git repo with changes made in the repo

option.name: --branch
tip: Fetch only some of the branches

option.name: --remote
tip: The remote to fetch from (only named remotes are supported, can be repeated)

next.name: import
tip: Update repo with changes made in the underlying Git repo

next.name: init
tip: Create a new Git backed repo

option.name: --colocate
tip: Whether or not to colocate the Jujutsu repo with the git repo.
It is the default value, and can be omitted.

option.name: --git-repo
tip: Specifies a path to an **existing** git repository to be used as the backing git repo for the newly created `jj` repo

option.name: --no-colocate
tip: Disable colocation of the Jujutsu repo with the git repo

option.name: --allow-empty-description
tip: Allow pushing commits with empty descriptions

option.name: --allow-private
tip: Allow pushing commits that are private

option.name: --bookmark
tip: Push only this bookmark, or bookmarks matching a pattern (can be repeated)

option.name: --change
tip: Push this commit by creating a bookmark (can be repeated)

option.name: --dry-run
tip: Only display what will change on the remote

option.name: --named
tip: Specify a new bookmark name and a revision to push under that name, e.g. '--named myfeature=@'

option.name: --remote
tip: The remote to push to (only named remotes are supported)

option.name: --revisions
tip: Push bookmarks pointing to these commits (can be repeated)

option.name: --fetch-tags
tip: Configure when to fetch tags [default: included]

next.name: remove
tip: Remove a Git remote and forget its bookmarks

next.name: set-url
tip: Set the URL of a Git remote

next.name: root
tip: Show the underlying Git directory of a repository using the Git backend

root.name: help
tip: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

root.name: interdiff
tip: Compare the changes of two commits

option.name: --limit
tip: Limit number of revisions to show

root.name: metaedit
tip: Modify the metadata of a revision without changing its content

option.name: --author
tip: Set author to the provided string

option.name: --author-timestamp
tip: Set the author date to the given date either human readable, eg Sun, 23 Jan 2000 01:23:45 CST) or as a time stamp, eg 2000-01-23T01:23:45+08:00)

option.name: --force-rewrite
tip: Rewrite the commit, even if no other metadata changed

option.name: --update-author
tip: Update the author to the configured user

root.name: new
tip: Create a new, empty change and (by default) edit it in the working copy

option.name: --insert-after
tip: Insert the new change after the given commit(s)

option.name: --insert-before
tip: Insert the new change before the given commit(s)

option.name: --no-edit
tip: Do not edit the newly created change

root.name: next
tip: Move the working-copy commit to the child revision

option.name: --conflict
tip: Jump to the next conflicted descendant

option.name: --edit
tip: Instead of creating a new working-copy commit on top of the target commit (like `jj new`), edit the target commit directly (like `jj edit`)
Takes precedence over config in `ui.movement.edit`; i.e. will negate `ui.movement.edit = false`

option.name: --no-edit
tip: Takes precedence over config in `ui.movement.edit`; i.e. will negate `ui.movement.edit = true`

root.name: operation
tip: Commands for working with the operation log

next.name: diff
tip: Compare changes to the repository between two operations

next.name: restore
tip: Create a new operation that restores the repo to an earlier state

next.name: revert
tip: Create a new operation that reverts an earlier operation

next.name: show
tip: Show changes to the repository in an operation

root.name: parallelize
tip: Parallelize revisions by making them siblings

root.name: prev
tip: Change the working copy revision relative to the parent revision

option.name: --branch
tip: Rebase the whole branch relative to destination's ancestors (can be repeated)

option.name: --insert-after
tip: The revision(s) to insert after (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --insert-before
tip: The revision(s) to insert before (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --onto
tip: The revision(s) to rebase onto (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --revisions
tip: Rebase the given revisions, rebasing descendants onto this revision's parent(s)

option.name: --skip-emptied
tip: If true, when rebasing would produce an empty commit, the commit is abandoned.
It will not be abandoned if it was already empty before the rebase.
Will never skip merge commits with multiple non-empty parents

option.name: --source
tip: Rebase specified revision(s) together with their trees of descendants (can be repeated)

root.name: redo
tip: Redo the most recently undone operation

root.name: resolve
tip: Resolve conflicted files with an external merge tool

option.name: --list
tip: Instead of resolving conflicts, list all the conflicts

option.name: --tool
tip: Specify 3-way merge tool to be used

root.name: revert
tip: Apply the reverse of the given revision(s)

root.name: root
tip: Show the current workspace root directory (shortcut for `jj workspace root`)

root.name: show
tip: Show commit description and changes in a revision

option.name: --color-words
tip: Show a word-level diff with changes indicated only by color

option.name: --context
tip: Number of lines of context to show

option.name: --ignore-space-change
tip: Ignore changes in amount of whitespace when comparing lines

option.name: --name-only
tip: For each path, show only its path

option.name: --stat
tip: Show a histogram of the changes

option.name: --summary
tip: For each path, show only whether it was modified, added, or deleted

option.name: --template
tip: Render a revision using the given template

option.name: --types
tip: For each path, show only its type before and after

root.name: simplify-parents
tip: Simplify parent edges for the specified revision(s)

root.name: sparse
tip: Manage which paths from the working-copy commit are present in the working copy

option.name: --interactive
tip: Interactively choose which parts to split

option.name: --tool
tip: Specify diff editor to be used (implies --interactive)

root.name: squash
tip: Move changes from a revision into another revision

option.name: --insert-after
tip: (Experimental) The revision(s) to insert the new commit after (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --insert-before
tip: (Experimental) The revision(s) to insert the new commit before (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --interactive
tip: Interactively choose which parts to squash

option.name: --keep-emptied
tip: The source revision will not be abandoned

option.name: --message
tip: The description to use for squashed revision (don't open editor)

option.name: --onto
tip: (Experimental) The revision(s) to use as parent for the new commit (can be repeated to create a merge commit)

option.name: --revisions
tip: Revision to squash into its parent (default: @).

option.name: --tool
tip: Specify diff editor to be used (implies --interactive)

option.name: --use-destination-message
tip: Use the description of the destination revision and discard the description(s) of the source revision(s)

option.name: --template
tip: Render each tag using the given template

root.name: util
tip: Infrequently used commands such as for generating shell completions

next.name: config-schema
tip: Print the JSON schema for the jj TOML config format

next.name: exec
tip: Execute an external command via jj

next.name: install-man-pages
tip: Install Jujutsu's manpages to the provided path

next.name: markdown-help
tip: Print the CLI help for all subcommands in Markdown

next.name: snapshot
tip: Snapshot the working copy if needed

next.name: forget
tip: Stop tracking a workspace's working-copy commit in the repo

next.name: update-stale
tip: Update a workspace that has become stale

common_option.name: --at-operation
tip: Operation to load the repo at

common_option.name: --ignore-working-copy
tip: Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it

common_option.name: --repository
tip: Path to repository to operate on
